user_query:
As a proud former student of Dr. Allen Amason, I want to ensure an upcoming alumni feature about him is accurate and fair. Please review the draft article against the supporting local materials and produce three outputs under the output/ directory:

Inputs to use (do not use external sources):
- input/draft_article.md
- input/course_outcomes.csv
- input/faculty_profiles.html

Deliverables:
1) output/evidence_summary.csv — a structured table where each row corresponds to a distinct claim you evaluated from the draft. Include the columns: claim_id, claim_text, evidence_source (course_outcomes.csv or faculty_profiles.html), metric_or_fact, computed_value, timeframe_assessed, status (one of: supported, partially supported, contradicted, unclear), notes (brief rationale). Ensure any numbers you report are recomputable from the inputs.
2) output/critique_report.md — a concise critique that:
   - Starts with an executive summary (key supported points and corrections needed).
   - For each claim, quotes the claim, states your judgment (supported/partially supported/contradicted/unclear), shows the evidence you extracted (with references to the specific file and fields/years), and proposes corrected wording when needed.
   - Concludes with a constructive note that reflects well on Dr. Amason while maintaining accuracy.
3) output/email_to_editor.txt — a polite, professional email draft to the College Communications Editor that:
   - Includes a clear subject line referencing the alumni feature about Dr. Allen Amason.
   - Summarizes the most important corrections (bullet points are fine) and points to the attached critique_report.md.
   - Uses an appreciative tone consistent with being a proud alumnus.

Assessment guidance and constraints (to make judgments consistent and verifiable from the local files):
- Base all judgments solely on the provided files; do not browse the web.
- For “since 2022” counts, include 2022, 2023, and 2024 data in your computation.
- For course evaluation averages of the undergraduate Strategic Management course, compute the mean of avg_rating for MGMT 4800 across the last three academic years represented in input/course_outcomes.csv.
- For “led three MBA capstone sections in Spring 2024,” count distinct sections where course_code == MGMT 7990, term == Spring, year == 2024, and instructor == Dr. Allen Amason.
- For titles/roles and awards, use the titles and awards listed under Dr. Allen C. Amason in input/faculty_profiles.html.
- For “publications in the past two years,” interpret the window as 2023–2024 and count Journal of Management items accordingly from input/faculty_profiles.html.

Make sure each deliverable is complete and internally consistent with the evidence_summary.csv. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
